requirements.summary: Configure VITE_GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID environment variable in the frontend build configuration to enable Google OAuth authentication
acceptanceCriteria: VITE_GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID environment variable is set in the frontend configuration
The error message 'Google Client ID not configured' no longer appears
The 'Connect Google Account' button initiates OAuth flow successfully

file_operations.description: Create example environment file documenting the VITE_GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID variable with placeholder value and instructions for obtaining a Google OAuth Client ID from Google Cloud Console
Create local environment file with VITE_GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID set to the actual Google OAuth Client ID value (this file should be gitignored and not committed)
Add .env to .gitignore if not already present to prevent committing sensitive OAuth credentials
Update the Google OAuth flow to read VITE_GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID from import.meta.env and ensure the error message displays correctly when the environment variable is missing
Ensure the component properly displays the configuration error from useYouTubeChannel hook and that the 'Connect Google Account' button is disabled when VITE_GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID is not configured

requirements.summary: Test the complete Google account connection flow from button click through OAuth callback to successful connection status display
acceptanceCriteria: User can click 'Connect Google Account' button
ChannelConnection component displays connected status with channel information
User can successfully post clips to YouTube after connection

file_operations.description: Enhance error handling and user feedback during OAuth callback processing, ensuring all OAuth state validation and credential storage steps provide clear success or failure messages
Verify the component correctly displays connected status with channel name and ID after successful OAuth flow, and ensure disconnect functionality works as expected
Add comprehensive error handling for the OAuth flow including redirect URI validation, state parameter verification, and clear error messages for common failure scenarios
Ensure the YouTube posting functionality properly checks for valid OAuth credentials before attempting to post clips, and provides clear feedback when authentication is required
